no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact fix: 'Configure the SVG minifier's KeepNamespaces option with the value []string{"", "x-bind"}', update to version v2.24.11, and add specific test cases with exact strings. This tells the agent exactly WHAT to change and HOW, leaving no reasoning required.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_config_has_keep_namespaces asserts the exact string 'KeepNamespaces: []string{"", "x-bind"}' in config.go. An alternative correct fix (e.g., different formatting, ordering the slice differently as ["x-bind", ""], or using a variable) would fail this text-matching test despite being functionally correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The three fail-to-pass tests (test_go_mod_dependencies_updated, test_config_has_keep_namespaces, test_minifiers_test_has_alpine_cases) are purely text-matching: they read files and assert exact strings are present. While pass-to-pass tests do execute code (go test, go build, go vet), the core fail-to-pass tests never execute the minifier or verify behavioral correctness directly.

For example, adding a comment containing 'KeepNamespaces: []string{"", "x-bind"}' to config.go would pass test_config_has_keep_namespaces. The behavioral tests (test_repo_minifiers_test) are pass-to-pass, not fail-to-pass.

The agent could read /solution/solve.sh or the test file test_outputs.py to see the exact expected strings and simply insert them.
